title: Phrygilanthus sonorae
taxonomy: Phrygilanthus sonorae belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This family consists of over 5,000 species of plants, commonly known as dogbanes or oleanders. The genus Phrygilanthus is a small group of flowering plants native to the Americas. Phrygilanthus sonorae is a species of flowering plant found in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Phrygilanthus sonorae is a part of the larger group of flowering plants, known as angiosperms.
